Interface ReactiveKeyCommands

All Known Subinterfaces:
ReactiveClusterKeyCommands
public interface ReactiveKeyCommands
Redis Key commands executed using reactive infrastructure.
Since:
2.0
Author:
Christoph Strobl, Mark Paluch, Dahye Anne Lee, Yordan Tsintsov

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.CopyCommand
    MOVE command parameters.
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.DelexCommand
    DELEX command parameters.
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand
    EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT command parameters.
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand
    EXPIRE/PEXPIRE command parameters.
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.MoveCommand
    MOVE command parameters.
    static class 
    ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand
    RENAME command parameters.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>>
    applyExpiration(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
    Expire a List of field after a given Duration of time, measured in milliseconds, has passed.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    copy(ByteBuffer sourceKey, ByteBuffer targetKey, boolean replace)
    Copy given key to a target key.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.CopyCommand>>
    copy(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.CopyCommand> commands)
    Copy keys one-by-one.
    default Mono<Long>
    del(ByteBuffer key)
    Delete key.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>>
    del(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
    Delete keys one by one.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    delex(ByteBuffer key, CompareCondition condition)
    Delete a key based on the provided CompareCondition and value.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.DelexCommand>>
    delex(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.DelexCommand> commands)
    Delete a key based on the provided CompareCondition.
    default Mono<String>
    digest(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the hash digest for the value stored in the specified key as a hexadecimal string.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.CommandResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, String>>
    digest(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
    Get the hash digest for the value stored in the specified key as a hexadecimal string.
    Mono<ValueEncoding>
    encodingOf(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the type of internal representation used for storing the value at the given key.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    exists(ByteBuffer key)
    Determine if given key exists.
    Mono<Long>
    exists(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
    Determine the number of given keys that exist.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand>>
    exists(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
    Determine if given key exists.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    expire(ByteBuffer key, Duration timeout)
    Set time to live for given key in seconds.
    default Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>>
    expire(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
    Expire keys one by one.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    expireAt(ByteBuffer key, Instant expireAt)
    Set the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand>>
    expireAt(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand> commands)
    Set one-by-one the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
    Mono<Duration>
    idletime(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the Duration since the object stored at the given key is idle.
    default Mono<List<ByteBuffer>>
    keys(ByteBuffer pattern)
    Retrieve all keys matching the given pattern via KEYS command.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.MultiValueResponse<ByteBuffer, ByteBuffer>>
    keys(Publisher<ByteBuffer> patterns)
    Find all keys matching the given pattern.
    It is recommended to use scan(ScanOptions) to iterate over the keyspace as keys(Publisher) is a non-interruptible and expensive Redis operation.
    default Mono<Long>
    mDel(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
    Delete multiple keys one in one batch.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<List<ByteBuffer>, Long>>
    mDel(Publisher<List<ByteBuffer>> keys)
    Delete multiple keys in batches.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    move(ByteBuffer key, int database)
    Move given key to database with index.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.MoveCommand>>
    move(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.MoveCommand> commands)
    Move keys one-by-one between databases.
    default Mono<Long>
    mUnlink(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
    Unlink the keys from the keyspace.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<List<ByteBuffer>, Long>>
    mUnlink(Publisher<List<ByteBuffer>> keys)
    Unlink the keys from the keyspace.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    persist(ByteBuffer key)
    Remove the expiration from given key.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand>>
    persist(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
    Remove one-by-one the expiration from given key.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    pExpire(ByteBuffer key, Duration timeout)
    Set time to live for given key in milliseconds.
    default Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>>
    pExpire(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
    Expire keys one by one.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    pExpireAt(ByteBuffer key, Instant expireAt)
    Set the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand>>
    pExpireAt(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand> commands)
    Set one-by-one the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.
    default Mono<Long>
    pTtl(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the time to live for key in milliseconds.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>>
    pTtl(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
    Get one-by-one the time to live for keys.
    Mono<ByteBuffer>
    randomKey()
    Return a random key from the keyspace.
    Mono<Long>
    refcount(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the number of references of the value associated with the specified key.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    rename(ByteBuffer oldKey, ByteBuffer newKey)
    Rename key oldKey to newKey.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand>>
    rename(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand> command)
    Rename key oldKey to newKey.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    renameNX(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer newKey)
    Rename key oldKey to newKey only if newKey does not exist.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand>>
    renameNX(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand> command)
    Rename key oldKey to newKey only if newKey does not exist.
    default Flux<ByteBuffer>
    scan()
    Use a Flux to iterate over keys.
    default Flux<ByteBuffer>
    scan(KeyScanOptions options)
    Use a Flux to iterate over keys.
    Flux<ByteBuffer>
    scan(ScanOptions options)
    Use a Flux to iterate over keys.
    default Mono<Long>
    touch(Collection<ByteBuffer> keys)
    Alter the last access time of given key(s).
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<Collection<ByteBuffer>, Long>>
    touch(Publisher<Collection<ByteBuffer>> keys)
    Alter the last access time of given key(s).
    default Mono<Long>
    ttl(ByteBuffer key)
    Get the time to live for key in seconds.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>>
    ttl(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
    Get one-by-one the time to live for keys.
    default Mono<DataType>
    type(ByteBuffer key)
    Determine the type stored at key.

    type(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
    Determine the type stored at key.
    default Mono<Long>
    unlink(ByteBuffer key)
    Unlink the key from the keyspace.
    Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>>
    unlink(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
    Unlink the key from the keyspace.

  • Method Details

    • copy

      default Mono<Boolean> copy(ByteBuffer sourceKey, ByteBuffer targetKey, boolean replace)
      Copy given key to a target key.
      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      targetKey - must not be null.
      replace - whether to replace existing keys.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: COPY

    • copy

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.CopyCommand>> copy(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.CopyCommand> commands)
      Copy keys one-by-one.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key to move along with the copy result.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: COPY

    • digest

      default Mono<String> digest(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the hash digest for the value stored in the specified key as a hexadecimal string. This command is intended to be used with string values only.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Mono emitting the digest string.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DIGEST

    • digest

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.CommandResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, String>> digest(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
      Get the hash digest for the value stored in the specified key as a hexadecimal string. This command is intended to be used with string values only.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.CommandResponse holding the key along with the digest.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DIGEST

    • exists

      default Mono<Boolean> exists(ByteBuffer key)
      Determine if given key exists.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXISTS

    • exists

      Mono<Long> exists(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
      Determine the number of given keys that exist.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null or empty.
      Returns:
      Mono emitting the number of existing keys.
      Since:
      3.5
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXISTS

    • exists

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand>> exists(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
      Determine if given key exists.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXISTS

    • type

      default Mono<DataType> type(ByteBuffer key)
      Determine the type stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TYPE

    • type

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.CommandResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, DataType>> type(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
      Determine the type stored at key.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TYPE

    • touch

      default Mono<Long> touch(Collection<ByteBuffer> keys)
      Alter the last access time of given key(s).
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Mono emitting the number of keys touched.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TOUCH

    • touch

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<Collection<ByteBuffer>, Long>> touch(Publisher<Collection<ByteBuffer>> keys)
      Alter the last access time of given key(s).
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TOUCH

    • keys

      default Mono<List<ByteBuffer>> keys(ByteBuffer pattern)
      Retrieve all keys matching the given pattern via KEYS command.

      IMPORTANT: This command is non-interruptible and scans the entire keyspace which may cause performance issues. Consider scan(ScanOptions) for large datasets.

      Parameters:
      pattern - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: KEYS

    • keys

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.MultiValueResponse<ByteBuffer, ByteBuffer>> keys(Publisher<ByteBuffer> patterns)
      Find all keys matching the given pattern.
      It is recommended to use scan(ScanOptions) to iterate over the keyspace as keys(Publisher) is a non-interruptible and expensive Redis operation.
      Parameters:
      patterns - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: KEYS

    • scan

      default Flux<ByteBuffer> scan()
      Use a Flux to iterate over keys. The resulting Flux acts as a cursor and issues SCAN commands itself as long as the subscriber signals demand.
      Returns:
      never null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCAN

    • scan

      default Flux<ByteBuffer> scan(KeyScanOptions options)
      Use a Flux to iterate over keys. The resulting Flux acts as a cursor and issues SCAN commands itself as long as the subscriber signals demand.
      Parameters:
      options - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Flux emitting keys one by one.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - when options is null.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCAN

    • scan

      Flux<ByteBuffer> scan(ScanOptions options)
      Use a Flux to iterate over keys. The resulting Flux acts as a cursor and issues SCAN commands itself as long as the subscriber signals demand.
      Parameters:
      options - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Flux emitting keys one by one.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - when options is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCAN

    • randomKey

      Mono<ByteBuffer> randomKey()
      Return a random key from the keyspace.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RANDOMKEY

    • rename

      default Mono<Boolean> rename(ByteBuffer oldKey, ByteBuffer newKey)
      Rename key oldKey to newKey.
      Parameters:
      oldKey - must not be null.
      newKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RENAME

    • rename

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand>> rename(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand> command)
      Rename key oldKey to newKey.
      Parameters:
      command - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RENAME

    • renameNX

      default Mono<Boolean> renameNX(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer newKey)
      Rename key oldKey to newKey only if newKey does not exist.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      newKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RENAMENX

    • renameNX

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand>> renameNX(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.RenameCommand> command)
      Rename key oldKey to newKey only if newKey does not exist.
      Parameters:
      command - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RENAMENX

    • del

      default Mono<Long> del(ByteBuffer key)
      Delete key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DEL

    • del

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>> del(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
      Delete keys one by one.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the key removed along with the deletion result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DEL

    • delex

      default Mono<Boolean> delex(ByteBuffer key, CompareCondition condition)
      Delete a key based on the provided CompareCondition and value.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      condition - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Mono.empty() if key did not exist.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DELEX

    • delex

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.DelexCommand>> delex(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.DelexCommand> commands)
      Delete a key based on the provided CompareCondition.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux emitting results when ready.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DELEX

    • mDel

      default Mono<Long> mDel(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
      Delete multiple keys one in one batch.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DEL

    • mDel

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<List<ByteBuffer>, Long>> mDel(Publisher<List<ByteBuffer>> keys)
      Delete multiple keys in batches.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the keys removed along with the deletion result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: DEL

    • unlink

      default Mono<Long> unlink(ByteBuffer key)
      Unlink the key from the keyspace. Unlike with del(ByteBuffer) the actual memory reclaiming here happens asynchronously.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: UNLINK

    • unlink

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>> unlink(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> keys)
      Unlink the key from the keyspace. Unlike with del(ByteBuffer) the actual memory reclaiming here happens asynchronously.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the key removed along with the unlink result.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: UNLINK

    • mUnlink

      default Mono<Long> mUnlink(List<ByteBuffer> keys)
      Unlink the keys from the keyspace. Unlike with mDel(List) the actual memory reclaiming here happens asynchronously.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: UNLINK

    • mUnlink

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<List<ByteBuffer>, Long>> mUnlink(Publisher<List<ByteBuffer>> keys)
      Unlink the keys from the keyspace. Unlike with mDel(Publisher) the actual memory reclaiming here happens asynchronously.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the key removed along with the deletion result.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: UNLINK

    • applyExpiration

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>> applyExpiration(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
      Expire a List of field after a given Duration of time, measured in milliseconds, has passed.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      a Flux emitting the expiration results one by one, true if the timeout was set or false if the timeout was not set; for example, the key doesn't exist, or the operation was skipped because of the provided arguments.
      Since:
      3.5
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXPIRE
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIRE
      • Redis Documentation: EXPIREAT
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIREAT
      • Redis Documentation: PERSIST

    • expire

      default Mono<Boolean> expire(ByteBuffer key, Duration timeout)
      Set time to live for given key in seconds.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXPIRE

    • expire

      default Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>> expire(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
      Expire keys one by one.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key removed along with the expiration result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXPIRE

    • pExpire

      default Mono<Boolean> pExpire(ByteBuffer key, Duration timeout)
      Set time to live for given key in milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIRE

      default Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand>> pExpire(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireCommand> commands)
      Expire keys one by one.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key removed along with the expiration result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIRE

      default Mono<Boolean> expireAt(ByteBuffer key, Instant expireAt)
      Set the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      expireAt - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EXPIREAT

      Set one-by-one the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key removed along with the expiration result.
      See Also:

      default Mono<Boolean> pExpireAt(ByteBuffer key, Instant expireAt)
      Set the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      expireAt - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIREAT

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand>> pExpireAt(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.ExpireAtCommand> commands)
      Set one-by-one the expiration for given key as a UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key removed along with the expiration result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PEXPIREAT

    • persist

      default Mono<Boolean> persist(ByteBuffer key)
      Remove the expiration from given key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PERSIST

    • persist

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand>> persist(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
      Remove one-by-one the expiration from given key.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key persisted along with the persist result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PERSIST

    • ttl

      default Mono<Long> ttl(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the time to live for key in seconds.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TTL

    • ttl

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>> ttl(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
      Get one-by-one the time to live for keys.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the key along with the time to live result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TTL

    • pTtl

      default Mono<Long> pTtl(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the time to live for key in milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: TTL

    • pTtl

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand, Long>> pTtl(Publisher<ReactiveRedisConnection.KeyCommand> commands)
      Get one-by-one the time to live for keys.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.NumericResponse holding the key along with the time to live result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: PTTL

    • move

      default Mono<Boolean> move(ByteBuffer key, int database)
      Move given key to database with index.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: MOVE

    • move

      Flux<ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse<ReactiveKeyCommands.MoveCommand>> move(Publisher<ReactiveKeyCommands.MoveCommand> commands)
      Move keys one-by-one between databases.
      Parameters:
      commands - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux of ReactiveRedisConnection.BooleanResponse holding the key to move along with the move result.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: MOVE

    • encodingOf

      Mono<ValueEncoding> encodingOf(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the type of internal representation used for storing the value at the given key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Mono emitting ValueEncoding.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if key is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: OBJECT ENCODING

    • idletime

      Mono<Duration> idletime(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the Duration since the object stored at the given key is idle.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Mono emitting the idletime of the key of Mono.empty() if the key does not exist.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if key is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: OBJECT IDLETIME

    • refcount

      Mono<Long> refcount(ByteBuffer key)
      Get the number of references of the value associated with the specified key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Mono.empty() if key does not exist.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if key is null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: OBJECT REFCOUNT
